requirement: the format specification separates rules from rationale

findings.observation: Binding rules carry RFC 2119 keywords while explanation does not, so a reader can tell a rule from its rationale; the Markdown-body and Appendix A example sections are explicitly marked OPTIONAL/non-normative so no rule lives only in an example.

In dense sections (Requirement, Analyze) obligations and rationale share long paragraphs; they remain distinguishable by keyword but require careful reading to separate, keeping this short of effortless separation.

rationale: Rules are consistently marked and never confined to examples, so binding force is always recoverable. Held at target rather than outstanding by dense passages that interleave rule and rationale in the same paragraph.
